name: AA Credit Union
description_short: AA Credit Union is a federally insured credit union offering checking, savings, auto loans, mortgages, and credit cards to members. Headquartered with a 90-year history, it serves individuals and businesses.
description_long:
AA Credit Union, officially American Airlines Federal Credit Union based on the website content, is a long-established credit union celebrating its 90th anniversary. The institution operates as a member-owned cooperative financial institution with ABA/Routing Number 311992904, providing traditional banking and lending services to a membership base.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking products. On the deposit side, members can access checking accounts (Priority Checking at 0.40% APY, Flagship Checking at 0.25% APY), savings accounts (up to 1.87% APY), and certificate products including short-term share certificates (4.05% APY), standard share certificates (4.00% APY), and ladder certificates (4.07% APY). For borrowing, AA Credit Union provides auto loans (as low as 3.74% APR for new autos up to 36 months), RV financing (6.99% APR), boat/airplane loans (6.74% APR), home loans (15-year fixed at 5.702% APR, 30-year fixed at 5.821% APR), and HELOCs (5/10 fixed at 7.250% APR). Credit card options include Signature Cash Back (14.25%-16.25% APR), Platinum Rewards (12.00%-17.74% APR), Platinum Low Rate (10.50%-18.00% APR), and Platinum Secured cards (15.74% APR). Business members can access business checking, business savings, and business credit cards.

AA Credit Union distinguishes itself through its credit union structure, offering member ownership benefits, a physical branch and ATM network, established history dating back decades, and competitive rates on auto loans (currently featuring a 90th anniversary promotion offering up to 0.90% APR discount). The institution maintains active fraud prevention awareness, warning members about spoofing scams and emphasizing security practices for online banking.

As a traditional credit union, AA Credit Union serves members seeking full-service banking with physical locations and member-owned governance. However, the website provides limited detail on membership eligibility criteria, fee structures, and specific business services. Deposit rates, while competitive, are in line with current market rates rather than exceptionally high. The credit union's online presence is functional but relatively basic compared to some digital-first financial institutions.
address: Miami International Airport, 2100 NW 20th St, Miami, FL 33126

company_info.headquarters: Miami International Airport, 2100 NW 20th St, Miami, FL 33126

pros: Competitive auto loan rates with 90th anniversary promotion offering up to 0.90% APR discount on new and used vehicles
Full-service credit union with multiple product lines (checking, savings, CDs, auto loans, mortgages, credit cards, business banking)
Certificate rates competitive at 4.00%-4.07% APY for share certificates and ladder certificates
Physical branch and ATM network with established 90-year operating history and member-owned structure
Home loan options across multiple term structures (15-year, 30-year, and ARM products) with rates starting at 5.702% APR
Dedicated business banking services including business checking, savings, and credit cards
Active fraud prevention and member security awareness (spoofing scam warnings)
cons: Checking account APYs are very low (0.25%-0.40%), offering minimal interest on balances
Credit card APRs range from 10.50%-18.00%, which are higher than some competitive card issuers
Website does not clearly disclose membership eligibility requirements or detailed fee schedules
Limited information about special lending products or alternative services (no mention of PALs or emergency lending programs)
Online banking platform appears basic with limited details about mobile app features or digital-first capabilities
best_for: Individuals seeking competitive auto financing with established credit history
Homebuyers comparing mortgage options with multiple term and rate structures
Small business owners needing integrated banking and credit card services
Members prioritizing physical branch access and member-owned credit union governance
diagnosis: AA Credit Union is best for individuals and small business owners seeking traditional full-service banking with competitive auto loan rates, mortgage options, and member-owned governance. The main caveat is that checking savings rates are minimal, credit card APRs are at the higher end of the market, and the institution lacks transparency about membership requirements and detailed fee structures on its public website.
services: Auto Loans for new and used vehicles (3.74%-4.99% APR)
Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOC, 7.250% APR)
Signature Cash Back Credit Cards (14.25%-16.25% APR)
Platinum Rewards and Low Rate Credit Cards (10.50%-18.00% APR)
